Module 3: Seed Launch, Part 1
!
Executive Summary
I know many of you have been waiting for this and are excited to learn the
process. The Seed Launch is a great place to start because you don’t need
a list or product.
It’s a very interactive process - this makes it easy to build relationships and
get feedback and case studies. It’s an easy, simple launch to do that can
lead to a much bigger launch.
In The Business Launch Formula you learned about how the PLF business
was started entirely from a seed launch. This is how you create a great
product right out of the gate.
Most of us are not trained teachers or trainers - we don’t instinctively know
how to teach correctly or appropriately.
There’s also something to be careful of that I call the “curse of knowledge”.
This is what happens when you forget the basics and are teaching about
something you’re an expert in.
You’re likely an expert in whatever you want to create a product about. As
an expert it’s very hard to remember what it was like to be a beginner.You
have so much knowledge and the beginner stuff is as simple as breathing
for you now
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.

-- 1 of 3 --

Example: when you were one year old walking was difficult, but you don’t
have to think about it anymore
The Seed Launch helps you avoid that “curse of knowledge”. It’s also very
interactive - lots of teleseminars or webinars.
Through surveys you perform before and after each call, you’ll find out what
they really want to know, where the gaps are, and what you need to revisit
or teach better.
This is how and why Product Launch Formula 1.0 was so great. The first 6
students from the Seed Launch gave the feedback needed. This made it
possible to get feedback that made the product better, but also made the
teaching more effective.
Now keep in mind, the Seed Launch is not just for beginners. If you have a
business but want to launch a new product, this is a fantastic way to get
started. It prevents procrastination because you get paid and are forced to
create the product (Eben Pagan calls this “inevitability thinking”).
I recently did a Seed Launch - it was a deep dive on a topic I’ve been
teaching for years. I learned so much more about my clients and where
they were at in their lives with all the PLF teachings.
It brought in money, created a great product, and lead to new learnings.
!
!
!

Checklist: When to Use a Seed Launch
You don’t have an email list
You don’t have a product
You’re just starting out and you’re a beginner
You have an idea for a product but aren’t sure it it will work
You don’t have experience teaching and need feedback
You need testimonials and case studies to prove your teaching works
You have a business and you want to launch a new product
You have a business and you want to make sure your new product is a
success
You want to get leverage on creating your product and get paid in
advance for it
You want to do a simple, easy launch
You want to make sure your customers actually want your product
You want to gauge demand for your new idea
!
